finding: Organism: Streptococcus pneumoniae, Haemophilus influenzae, Staphylococcus aureus

differentials.name: retroauricular swelling + erythema, protrusion of auricle
differentials.raw: retroauricular swelling + erythema, protrusion of auricle

differentials.name: Coalescent mastoiditis = empyema of mastoid Pathomechanism:   suppuration under pressure → local acidosis + ischemia → osseous decalcification + osteoclasis of pneumatic cell walls
differentials.raw: Coalescent mastoiditis = empyema of mastoid Pathomechanism:   suppuration under pressure → local acidosis + ischemia → osseous decalcification + osteoclasis of pneumatic cell walls

differentials.name: Carotid artery involvement: arteritis, occlusion, pseudoaneurysm, rupture
differentials.raw: Carotid artery involvement: arteritis, occlusion, pseudoaneurysm, rupture

differentials.name: Bezold abscess = inflammatory collection that may spread along plane of sternocleidomastoid muscle to lower neck Location:   inferior to mastoid tip + medial to insertion of posterior belly of digastric muscle
differentials.raw: Bezold abscess = inflammatory collection that may spread along plane of sternocleidomastoid muscle to lower neck Location:   inferior to mastoid tip + medial to insertion of posterior belly of digastric muscle

differentials.name: Epidural abscess ›  perisinus abscess = erosion of the cortical plate overlying the sigmoid sinus
differentials.raw: Epidural abscess ›  perisinus abscess = erosion of the cortical plate overlying the sigmoid sinus

context: Temporal bone CT (87%-100% sensitive): √  increased attenuation of the middle ear cavity (= middle ear effusion) √  increased attenuation of the mastoid cells √  ± fluid levels Intratemporal Cx: √  loss of internal bone septa of the mastoid air cells ← erosion of mastoid septa / cortex with periosteal reaction √  fluid or soft-tissue opacification of mastoid air cells √  subperiosteal abscess located postauricular / in external auditory canal / along zygomatic bone Rx:   myringotomy, mastoidectomy √  abnormal labyrinthine enhancement Cervical Cx: √  erosion of mastoid tip √  adjacent involveme
